title: pilea aquarum subsp. acutidentata
intro: Pilea aquarum subsp. acutidentata is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and short stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and vasodilatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pilea aquarum subsp. acutidentata are typically made by boiling the leaves and stems in water to create a decoction, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions.
